nodes.title: On Connect
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires once when the bot finishes registering with the server.

nodes.title: On Signal
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires when another part of your bot emits a matching signal (via Emit Signal). Lets one workflow trigger another, or run a shared flow from several places. Carries optional {data}.

nodes.title: Emit Signal
nodes.subtitle: flow
nodes.description: Triggers every On Signal node with the matching name, running their flows now. A way for one workflow to call another. Pass optional data along.

nodes.title: On Message
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires for every channel/PM message. Optionally limit to one channel.

nodes.title: On Command
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires when a message begins with a prefix + command word, e.g. !ping.

nodes.title: On Join
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires when a user joins a channel the bot is in.

nodes.title: On Numeric
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires when the server sends a numeric reply you pick from the list (e.g. RPL_WELCOME, ERR_NICKNAMEINUSE, RPL_INVITING). Exposes {numeric} {numname} {message} {channel} and {arg1}, {arg2}, …

nodes.title: Text Contains
nodes.subtitle: filter
nodes.description: Branches on whether the text contains a substring.

nodes.title: From User
nodes.subtitle: filter
nodes.description: Branches on whether the event came from a specific nick.

nodes.title: From Account
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Branches on the sender's authenticated account (IRCv3 account-tag) - a safe allow-list, since accounts can't be faked the way nicks can. Blank = anyone logged in; one account, or a comma-separated allow-list.

params.label: Account(s)
params.placeholder: blank = any logged-in · or alice · or alice, bob, carol

nodes.title: Is Bot
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Branches on whether the sender is flagged as a bot (IRCv3 bot mode/tag).

nodes.title: Random Chance
nodes.subtitle: filter
nodes.description: Branches randomly: 'hit' with the given percent chance.

nodes.title: Random Reply
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Outputs a random line from the list (one option per line).

nodes.title: Format Text
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Builds a string from a template. {a}/{b} use the inputs; {nick} etc. use the event.

nodes.title: Send Reply
nodes.subtitle: action
nodes.description: Replies in the channel/PM the triggering message came from.

nodes.title: Send to Channel
nodes.subtitle: action
nodes.description: Sends a PRIVMSG to a specific channel or nick.

nodes.title: Join Channel
nodes.subtitle: action
nodes.description: Makes the bot join a channel.

nodes.title: Part Channel
nodes.subtitle: action
nodes.description: Makes the bot leave a channel. Blank channel = the triggering channel.

nodes.title: Raw IRC
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Sends a raw IRC line, with up to 4 IRCv3 client-tags composed for you. Tag keys may omit the leading '+'. The line is the rest of the protocol, e.g. PRIVMSG #lobby :lmao

nodes.title: Start Typing
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Shows an IRCv3 typing indicator (+typing=active) on the target, refreshed every few seconds until Stop Typing or the workflow ends. Great before a slow AI reply. Needs a server with message-tags.

nodes.title: Stop Typing
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Stops the IRCv3 typing indicator (+typing=done) on the target.

nodes.title: Add Reaction
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Reacts to the triggering message with an emoji (IRCv3 +draft/react). Needs a server that supports message tags.

nodes.title: Reply (threaded)
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Replies threaded to the triggering message (IRCv3 +draft/reply), so clients show it as a reply.

nodes.title: Set Name
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Changes the bot's realname live (IRCv3 setname). No reconnect needed on servers that support it.

nodes.title: Set Away
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Sets or clears the bot's away status (away-notify / pre-away). Blank message = back.

nodes.title: Send TAGMSG
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Sends a tags-only message (IRCv3 TAGMSG) - client tags with no text, e.g. a reaction or typing hint.

nodes.title: Redact Message
nodes.subtitle: draft
nodes.description: Deletes/hides a message by id (draft/message-redaction REDACT). Needs the target message's id (msgid).

nodes.title: Monitor User
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Watches nicks for online/offline (IRCv3 MONITOR). Add or remove a comma-separated list.

nodes.title: Request History
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Fetches a target's recent server history (IRCv3 CHATHISTORY), INCLUDING messages from before the bot joined. The history batch never triggers your message/join nodes - it just hands the messages back as JSON on 'messages'. Wire that into For Each, or into Ask AI as a tool. Each item has nick, channel, text and id.

nodes.title: My Info
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Reads the bot's own live, tracked state: its nick, the network it's connected to, the channels it's in (comma-separated), or its enabled IRCv3 caps. Wire 'value' anywhere.

nodes.title: Channel Info
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Reads live, tracked state about a channel the bot is in: its topic, member list (comma-separated, with @/+ prefixes), member count, or whether the bot is in it. Wire 'value' anywhere.

nodes.title: Rename Channel
nodes.subtitle: draft
nodes.description: Renames a channel in place (draft/channel-rename RENAME), keeping members. Needs the right privileges.

nodes.title: Set Metadata
nodes.subtitle: draft
nodes.description: Sets a metadata key on a target (draft/metadata METADATA SET), e.g. an avatar or status URL.

nodes.title: Send Multiline
nodes.subtitle: draft
nodes.description: Sends several lines as one logical message (draft/multiline batch). One line per row in the text.

nodes.title: Console Log
nodes.subtitle: action
nodes.description: Writes a line to the ircuitry event console.

nodes.title: Ask AI
nodes.subtitle: ai
nodes.description: Generates a reply via any OpenAI-compatible API (OpenAI, Ollama, LM Studio, OpenRouter, Groq, vLLM…). Wire AI Tool nodes into 'tools' to let the model fetch data / act. Wire 'reply' into a Send Reply.

params.label: System prompt
params.default: You are a witty IRC bot named ircuitry. Reply in one short sentence.

nodes.title: Recent Messages
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Lists the messages the bot has recently seen, as JSON [{nick,channel,text,id}]. As an AI tool it lets the model find a message to act on.

nodes.title: React to Message
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Adds an emoji reaction to a specific message by its id (from Recent Messages / Request History). As an AI tool the model passes the id + emoji it chose.

nodes.title: AI Tool
nodes.subtitle: ai
nodes.description: Defines a tool the AI may call, with ANY number of arguments (Add argument). Wire 'tool' into Ask AI; wire 'call' to a sub-flow that ends in a Tool Reply. Read an argument anywhere with {arg.NAME} - the first three are also on the arg pins.

params.label: What it does
params.placeholder: tell the model when to use it

nodes.title: Tool Reply
nodes.subtitle: ai
nodes.description: Ends an AI Tool's sub-flow: whatever you feed it is the result the model gets back.

nodes.title: Workflow Editor
nodes.subtitle: ai
nodes.description: Gives an Ask AI node the inward MCP tools to read and EDIT bot workflows in this workspace - its own or another bot's: add/remove nodes, wire pins, set params, replace or validate a graph, dry-run a command. Wire 'tools' into Ask AI. Edits save to the workspace (the canvas hot-reloads); a running bot applies them on its next start.

params.label: Target bot
params.placeholder: blank = the active bot; or a bot name (the model can still target others by name)

nodes.title: Programmer AI
nodes.subtitle: ai
nodes.description: An AI software engineer that reads and edits a whole codebase (read/write/edit/search/move files, run build & test commands) and delivers the finished result. It is sandboxed to the codebase folder you give it and CANNOT touch anything outside. Set a 'task' and wire 'reply' into Send Reply. Wire extra AI Tool nodes into 'tools' to give it more abilities.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · the AI is locked inside this folder

params.label: Filehost URL
params.placeholder: where the finished codebase is uploaded

nodes.title: Alert Human
nodes.subtitle: notify
nodes.description: Pops a native desktop notification on the machine running ircuitry, so a human notices something even when they aren't watching chat. notify-send on Linux, osascript on macOS, a toast on Windows (best effort).

nodes.title: Human in the Loop
nodes.subtitle: approval
nodes.description: Pauses the flow and asks a human to approve or deny before it continues (like n8n's Human in the Loop). Posts the question to a channel/PM (and optionally a desktop alert), then resumes on 'approved' or 'denied' when they reply - or 'denied' if it times out. Their reply text comes out on 'response'.

params.label: Ask in
params.placeholder: channel or nick (blank = where the event came from)

params.label: Only from
params.placeholder: a specific nick, or blank for anyone there

nodes.title: HTTP Request
nodes.subtitle: web
nodes.description: Calls a web API. Sends a text/JSON body, or uploads a file as multipart/form-data (the standard way to push an image or any file to a filehost). Outputs the response body and status. Headers are 'Key: value' lines.

params.label: Body / form fields
params.placeholder: { ... } for POST  ·  or key=value lines for multipart

nodes.title: Read File
nodes.subtitle: file
nodes.description: Reads a text file and outputs its contents. Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files. Branches to 'missing' if the file isn't there.

nodes.title: Write File
nodes.subtitle: file
nodes.description: Writes (or appends) text to a file. Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files, which is created if missing.

nodes.title: Calendar (iCal)
nodes.subtitle: calendar
nodes.description: Reads an iCalendar (.ics) feed - a file path, an http(s) URL, or pasted text - and pulls out events. Modes: next upcoming, today's, a count, or a list. Outputs summary/when/location/description; branches to 'none' when empty.

nodes.title: Read File
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Reads a text file from the codebase and returns its contents (optionally a line range). Paths are relative to the codebase folder. Branches to 'missing' if the file isn't there.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Write File
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Creates or overwrites a file with the given content (parent folders are made). Use for new files or full rewrites; prefer Edit File for small changes. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Edit File
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Replaces an exact piece of text in a file with new text - the precise way to change code. By default 'find' must be unique (one edit); turn on Replace all to change every occurrence. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Insert Lines
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Inserts text into a file after a given line number (0 = at the very top). Good for adding imports, functions or config blocks. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Append to File
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Appends text to the end of a file (creating it if needed). Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Delete Path
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Deletes a file or a folder (recursively) inside the codebase. Refuses to delete the codebase root itself. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Move / Rename
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Moves or renames a file or folder within the codebase. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Copy Path
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Copies a file or folder (recursively) to a new path within the codebase. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Make Directory
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Creates a directory (and any missing parents) inside the codebase. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: List Directory
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Lists the files and subfolders directly inside a directory (folders end with /). Paths are relative to the codebase folder; blank lists the root.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Project Tree
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Shows a compact directory tree (skipping .git, node_modules, build output, etc.) so an AI can orient itself in the codebase. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Find Files
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Finds files by glob pattern (e.g. **/*.cs, src/*.py, **/test_*.py), newest first. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Search Code
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Searches the codebase for a regular expression and returns matches as path:line: text. Optionally restrict to files matching a glob. The fast way to find where something is defined or used.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Replace Across Files
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Replaces an exact string with another across every matching file (optionally filtered by a glob). Returns the number of files changed. Powerful - keep the find text specific.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: File Info
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Reports whether a path is a file or folder, plus size, line count and last-modified time. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Path Exists
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Checks whether a file or folder exists in the codebase. Branches yes/no and returns true/false. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Diff Files
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Shows the line differences between two files in the codebase (- removed, + added). Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Run Command
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Runs a shell command with its working directory set to the codebase folder (build, test, lint, git, etc.) and returns the combined output. The working directory is confined to the codebase.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Code Outline
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Lists the top-level definitions (classes, functions, methods) in a source file as line: signature, so an AI can navigate large files. Paths are relative to the codebase folder.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Project Stats
nodes.subtitle: code
nodes.description: Summarises the codebase: file count, total lines, size, and the most common file types. A quick overview before diving in.

params.label: Codebase folder
params.placeholder: ~/projects/mybot · all paths are relative to this and can't escape it

nodes.title: Image Info
nodes.subtitle: media
nodes.description: Reads an image's width, height and format straight from its header (PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP, WebP) - no decoding, no dependencies. Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files. Branches to 'not image' if unrecognised.

nodes.title: Download File
nodes.subtitle: media
nodes.description: Downloads a URL (image, audio, any file) to a local file under ~/ircuitry/files and outputs the saved path. Branches to 'failed' on error.

nodes.title: Organize Media
nodes.subtitle: media
nodes.description: Moves, copies, renames or deletes a media file under ~/ircuitry/files - handy for sorting downloads into folders.

nodes.title: Transform Image
nodes.subtitle: media
nodes.description: Resizes, converts or rotates an image using ImageMagick or ffmpeg if installed. Outputs the new file's path; branches to 'failed' (with a clear message) when no image tool is available. Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files.

nodes.title: Zip
nodes.subtitle: archive
nodes.description: Compresses a folder (or a single file) into a .zip archive. Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files; absolute paths are honoured. As an AI tool, pass a source path and a zip path.

nodes.title: Unzip
nodes.subtitle: archive
nodes.description: Extracts a .zip archive into a folder (created if needed), safely rejecting any entry that would escape the folder (zip-slip). Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files.

nodes.title: Encode / Decode
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Encodes or decodes text: Base64, Base32, hex, URL, HTML entities, binary, Morse, or ROT13.

nodes.title: Hash
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Hashes text with MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512, or a CRC32 checksum.

nodes.title: Change Case
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Recases text: UPPER, lower, Title, Sentence, camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case, mOcKiNg, l33t, clap👏case, and more.

nodes.title: Shape Text
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Reshapes text: reverse, trim, squeeze spaces, length, repeat, truncate, pad, slugify, acronym, disemvowel, number lines, word-wrap.

nodes.title: Regex
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Runs a regular expression over text: test match, extract the first match / capture group, extract all matches, count, or replace.

nodes.title: Math
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Evaluates an arithmetic expression: + - * / % ^, parentheses, and functions (sqrt, abs, round, floor, ceil, sin, cos, log, …). Supports pi and e.

nodes.title: Unit Convert
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Converts a value between units: temperature (c/f/k), length (mm..mi), mass (mg..lb), speed (m/s, km/h, mph, kn), data (B..TiB).

nodes.title: Number Theory
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Whole-number math: factorial, nth Fibonacci, GCD, LCM, is-prime, next prime, ordinal (1st/2nd), absolute value, sign.

nodes.title: Number Format
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Formats a number: convert between bases (bin/oct/dec/hex/any), Roman numerals (and back), number-to-words, or thousands separators.

nodes.title: Date / Time
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Works with dates/times: current time (any timezone), format a date, day of week, unix timestamp conversion, countdown until a date, or age in years.

nodes.title: Random Generator
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Generates random things: a coin flip, dice (NdM+K), a UUID, a password, a cute username, a fake name, a hex colour, an integer in a range, or lorem ipsum.

nodes.title: Pick from List
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Picks from a list: a random item, the first / last / Nth, or counts them. Choose how the list is separated.

nodes.title: Number Stats
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Computes a statistic over a list of numbers found in the text: sum, mean, median, min, max, count, range, or standard deviation.

nodes.title: IRC Color
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Adds or removes IRC formatting: rainbow-colour text, strip all colours/formatting, or wrap in bold / italic / underline.

nodes.title: On DCC Offer
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires when a user offers a DCC transfer over IRC - a direct file SEND, or a CHAT/RESUME/ACCEPT negotiation. Decide what to do with Accept DCC File or Decline DCC. The 'account' pin is the sender's authenticated login (use it to allow-list - nicks can be faked, accounts can't). Also exposes {dcc.file} {dcc.size} {dcc.ip} {dcc.port} {dcc.token} {dcc.type} {account}.

nodes.title: Accept DCC File
nodes.subtitle: dcc
nodes.description: Accepts the DCC file offer that triggered this flow and downloads it - you choose where it lands. Blank path = ~/ircuitry/files/dcc/. A folder saves it there under the offered name; a full path names the file. The transfer runs in the background and logs when it finishes.

params.label: Save to (file or folder)
params.placeholder: blank = ~/ircuitry/files/dcc/ · or a folder · or a full path

nodes.title: Decline DCC
nodes.subtitle: dcc
nodes.description: Declines the DCC offer that triggered this flow (DCC has no formal 'no' - we simply don't connect, and optionally send the sender a notice).

nodes.title: Send File (DCC)
nodes.subtitle: dcc
nodes.description: Offers a file to a user over DCC: the bot listens on a port, sends the DCC SEND, and streams the file when they accept. Relative paths live under ~/ircuitry/files. Note: the receiver connects back to this machine, so a public IP / port-forwarding may be needed across the internet.

nodes.title: Get Tag
nodes.subtitle: ircv3
nodes.description: Reads any IRCv3 message tag from the triggering message (e.g. account, time, msgid).

nodes.title: JSON Field
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Extracts a value from JSON by dotted path, e.g. results.0.title.

nodes.title: On Timer
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires every N seconds while the bot is connected. Pair with Send to Channel for announcements.

nodes.title: On Schedule
nodes.subtitle: trigger
nodes.description: Fires on a schedule: an interval, every day at a time, on chosen weekdays, or once at a date/time. Uses your computer's local time. Outputs {time} and {date}; pair with Send to Channel.

nodes.title: If / Compare
nodes.subtitle: condition
nodes.description: Compares A to B and branches. A defaults to the message; B uses the input if wired, else the value field.

nodes.title: Regex Match
nodes.subtitle: condition
nodes.description: Matches text against a pattern; branches and outputs capture groups $1 and $2.

nodes.title: Switch
nodes.subtitle: logic
nodes.description: Routes to the output whose case matches the value, else 'default'. Add as many cases as you like with 'Add case' - each one grows its own output pin. Great for command menus. (The 'default' pin stays on top so adding a case never disturbs your existing wires.)

nodes.title: Cooldown
nodes.subtitle: logic
nodes.description: Only passes once per N seconds (optionally per-user). Stops spam and rate-limits commands.

nodes.title: For Each
nodes.subtitle: logic
nodes.description: Runs 'each' once per list item, then 'done'. Splits text by a separator, or set Separator to 'json' to iterate a JSON array. Each item is on the 'item' pin and in a var (default {item}) so {item.field} works for JSON elements.

nodes.title: Repeat
nodes.subtitle: logic
nodes.description: Runs 'each' a fixed number of times (the 0-based count on 'i' and as {i}), then 'done'. Bounded so it can never loop forever.

nodes.title: Set Variable
nodes.subtitle: state
nodes.description: Stores a value under a name. Persists across events and saves with the bot.

nodes.title: Get Variable
nodes.subtitle: state
nodes.description: Reads a stored variable (or the default if it isn't set yet).

nodes.title: Math
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Computes A (op) B as a number. Pair with variables for counters and scores.

nodes.title: Text Transform
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: Transforms text: lower / upper / trim / length / reverse / replace.

nodes.title: Random Number
nodes.subtitle: data
nodes.description: A random whole number between min and max (inclusive). For !roll, !8ball, etc.

nodes.title: Set Topic
nodes.subtitle: channel
nodes.description: Sets a channel's topic (needs the right channel privileges). Blank channel = the triggering channel.

nodes.title: Kick User
nodes.subtitle: moderation
nodes.description: Kicks a user from a channel (needs operator). Blank channel = the triggering channel.

nodes.title: Set Mode
nodes.subtitle: moderation
nodes.description: Applies a channel mode, e.g. +o/-o (op), +v (voice), +b (ban). Target is a nick or mask; leave blank for channel-wide modes like +m.

nodes.title: Send Action
nodes.subtitle: emote
nodes.description: Sends a CTCP ACTION (a /me emote), e.g. "waves hello". Replies to the triggering channel/user.

nodes.title: Delay
nodes.subtitle: flow
nodes.description: Waits before continuing. Runs off the connection thread, so it never stalls keepalive or other workflows (capped at 5 min). For long or recurring waits, prefer a Schedule/Timer trigger.

nodes.title: Subflow Start
nodes.subtitle: subflow
nodes.description: The entry point of a reusable subflow. Wire its 'then' to the subflow's body. (Only meaningful inside a saved node.)

nodes.title: Subflow Input
nodes.subtitle: subflow
nodes.description: Reads one of the subflow's named inputs. The 'name' becomes an input pin on the saved node.

nodes.title: Subflow Output
nodes.subtitle: subflow
nodes.description: Writes one of the subflow's named outputs. The 'name' becomes an output pin on the saved node.

nodes.title: DB Set
nodes.subtitle: database
nodes.description: Stores a value in a named table on disk (~/ircuitry/data). Empty value deletes the key. Survives restarts and is shared across bots.

nodes.title: DB Get
nodes.subtitle: database
nodes.description: Reads from a named table on disk. Modes: a key's value, the row count, or a substring search across values.

nodes.title: AI Memory
nodes.subtitle: ai
nodes.description: Per-conversation memory for Ask AI. 'recall' outputs the running transcript to feed into the prompt; 'remember' appends a turn; 'clear' wipes it. Keyed by session (default the channel), kept to the last N turns, and persisted.

nodes.title: Add Calendar Event
nodes.subtitle: calendar
nodes.description: Appends an event to an .ics file (created if missing). Start accepts '2026-12-31 09:00' or a date; duration is in minutes.

nodes.title: Search Calendar
nodes.subtitle: calendar
nodes.description: Searches an .ics source (file/URL/text) for events whose title, location or notes contain the query, optionally within a date range. Branches to 'none' if nothing matches.

nodes.title: SQL Query
nodes.subtitle: database
nodes.description: Advanced: runs raw SQL against a SQLite file (created on first use). SELECT returns rows (pipe-separated); INSERT/UPDATE/DDL return the affected count.

params.label: SQL
params.placeholder: SELECT * FROM scores ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 5;

nodes.title: Code
nodes.subtitle: js / python
nodes.description: Runs JavaScript (node) or Python (python3). Reads context from env vars (NICK, CHANNEL, MESSAGE, ARGS, INPUT) or JSON on stdin; whatever it prints to stdout becomes 'output'.
